historicalEvidence.result: invalidated for protected plan-path exposure; not scored
invalidated at local classification boundary before authorization; not scored

protectedPathPolicy.canonicalization: case-insensitive slash, backslash, and dotted-module normalization

cases.label: retained classification-boundary details-state behavior
cases.boundaryPurpose: Retains the fixture file-tree shape that previously exposed a protected baseline prefix during Luna classification.
visibleAcceptanceCriteria: Opening response details makes the panel visible and sets aria-expanded to true.
Closing response details hides the panel and sets aria-expanded to false.
Create and independently execute a candidate-authored unittest under the documented generated-test location.
Treat the requested behavior as client-only and do not invent a production layer.

cases.label: fresh incident-owner endpoint requiring planner correction
visibleAcceptanceCriteria: GET /incident-owner/:incident_id returns status 200 for a known incident.
The success body contains exactly id and owner.
A missing incident returns status 404 with the stable error body.
The initial deliberately incomplete service-only plan is rejected, and planner correction restores the API, service, generated-test, backing-unit, and public-handler surfaces before generation.
The repository module and existing verification-only suites remain unchanged.
Create and independently execute a candidate-authored unittest under the documented generated-test location.

predeclaredSuccessCriteria: Every outbound classification, initial-planning, planner-correction, generation, generation-repair, validation-repair, and verification-repair request that occurs records a passing protected-path assertion before authorization and before provider transport.
No model-visible prompt, selected context, downstream plan, repair error, or generated-test feedback contains a canonical protected path or hidden verification content.
Both automatic routes exactly match the expected model and reasoning effort without a route, model, or reasoning override.
The fresh case records a planner-correction request after the deliberately incomplete initial plan and uses the corrected complete plan for generation.
Neither candidate invents a production layer outside the fixture's existing architecture.
No repair is driven by any removed false diagnostic listed in forbiddenRepairDiagnostics.
The visible acceptance contract and hidden verification both pass for each case.
Usage, reservations, latency, retries, route, validation, verification, repair, and generated-test evidence persist without prompts, hidden contents, secrets, or API keys.
Observed cost, outstanding reservations, and committed cost reconcile; committed cost never exceeds 3 USD and each OpenAI reservation uses the frozen 49152-token minimum.
The denominator remains exactly one paid trial for each of the two frozen cases; no failure, timeout, refusal, invalidation, or weak response is rerun, replaced, or rescored.
forbiddenRepairDiagnostics: An unchanged JavaScript companion is treated as a mandatory edit.
Client-only interaction is treated as backend work.
Generated-test wording is treated as full-stack production work.
Python test declarations are treated as application calls.
integrityStopRules: Stop and invalidate immediately if any outbound request boundary assertion rejects a protected canonical path.
Stop and invalidate immediately if hidden verification content or failure detail enters model-visible context.
Do not rerun any failure, timeout, refusal, budget stop, poor model response, or integrity-invalidated trial.
After the paid command, do not change production code until the immutable raw proof and report are committed.
Retain every result and interruption without replacement, rescue, or rescoring.
requiredReporting: Both raw and final case results, expected and actual routes, route accuracy, raw pass@1, repair-assisted outcome, and final pass@1; metrics are null rather than false when no candidate exists.
Every content-free request-boundary assertion phase and status, including the required planner correction.
Generated-test paths and independent execution outcomes, visible checks, hidden verification, validation, verification, and repair histories.
Model, validator, repair, oracle, provider, budget, and evaluation-integrity failure classification.
Per-case and aggregate calls, tokens, latency, retries, reservations, observed cost, outstanding reservations, committed cost, and unknown canceled-call exposure.
Frozen hashes, exact commands, implementation commit, proof-freeze commit, proof-artifact commit, and remaining risks.
